decision_tree.description: Choose the right architectural style for your system

start.question: What's the team size and operational maturity?

answer.rationale: The default for new systems. One deployable unit, but clear internal module boundaries. Premature microservices is one of the most expensive mistakes.

answer.pick: Modular Monolith with future extraction in mind
answer.rationale: Same as above, but be more aggressive about module boundaries. Plan for extraction but don't do it yet.
Separate read/write models. Event-sourced aggregates. Replay events for new read models. Powerful but operationally complex — apply selectively, not globally.
Citus, Vitess, or CockroachDB. Shard by tenant or entity. Stay relational as long as possible.
Producers publish events. Consumers react. Use Kafka for durable log. Async by default. Test event schemas.
Local storage as source of truth. Background sync. Conflict-free replicated data types. Hard but worth it for offline UX.
Each region serves writes. Conflicts resolved by CRDT, vector clock, or last-write-wins. Expensive. Use only when necessary.
Small, focused services. Istio/Linkerd for cross-cutting. Each team owns a service. Requires platform team.
